name: George Fassett's Fake Jerky Strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix ground beef with all other ingredients in a bowl until well combined.
2. Shape the mixture into an 8-inch by 12-inch rectangle, no more than 1/8 inch thick.
3. Using a pizza cutter, slice into 1-inch wide strips that are 8 inches long.
4. Carefully transfer the strips to a wire rack.
5. Preheat oven to 300°F and place the wire rack with meat on an oven rack in the center of the oven. Place a cookie sheet under the meat on a lower rack to catch drippings.
6. Bake for 1 hour at 300°F.
7. Mix sauce ingredients in a shallow dish. Remove the rack from the oven, roll each strip into the sauce mixture, and return to the rack. Increase temperature to 450°F and bake for 15 minutes.
8. Remove the rack again, recoat the strips, and return them to the oven for another 15 minutes or until the edges are nearly burnt.
9. Take the rack out of the oven, coat the strips one last time, and let cool to room temperature before serving.
Enjoy!
